text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 12 April 2020 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Emma Carter, DOB: 23-08-1987, Hospital No: U918273 NHS No. 3049857612  
Flat 4, 12 Brookfield Road, London, NW1 6AB 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy (left temporal semiology suspected)/post-concussive headaches now resolved/ketogenic-modified diet under external supervision/possible mild anxiety related to COVID-19 disruptions. 

Medication: Lamotrigine 100 mg twice a day (restarted 12-Mar).  
Clobazam 10 mg at night as required for clusters (not used in the last three weeks).  
Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily.  
Folic acid 5 mg once daily (per preconception counselling previously, continued by patient preference). 

Dietary management: Patient is following a ketogenic/modified low-carbohydrate regimen supervised by an external dietetics team. Reported daily macronutrient targets: fat 65–70%, protein 20–25%, carbohydrate 5–10% (approximately 20–30 g net carbs/day). Uses medium-chain triglyceride (MCT) oil 10 mL with breakfast and dinner. Checks blood ketones at home 3–4 times per week (typical 0.8–1.6 mmol/L). Hydration goal ≥2 L/day; electrolyte supplementation with magnesium citrate 200 mg nocte and sodium broth on active days. No reported adverse effects other than transient constipation managed with psyllium husk. 

I reviewed her in clinic today via scheduled telephone follow-up owing to current service constraints. She was alone for the call and had her diet log and seizure diary to hand. Energy levels are described as "improving" since mid-March, and sleep is averaging 7–8 hours nightly. She reports good adherence to Lamotrigine since reinitiation and has not required rescue Clobazam. 

Seizures: She had recurrent headaches following her seizures earlier this month, but these have now settled. On 12-Mar the absence episodes reduced significantly after she restarted Lamotrigine. Her last reported event was on 19-Mar and she has been seizure-free since. As she has now been stable for over three weeks, I am reassured and see no need for urgent changes. 

Examination/observations: Not examined in person today. Over the phone she was alert and fully oriented, conversational speech fluent, no word-finding pauses reported by patient. She denies new focal neurological symptoms, visual auras, or deja vu phenomena since 19-Mar. No injuries or falls. Weight by home scale 62.4 kg (stable). Blood pressure last recorded in community check two weeks ago 118/72 mmHg, pulse 68 bpm regular (patient-reported). 

Investigations: No new tests today. Prior MRI (2018) non-lesional. Prior routine EEG (2019) with intermittent left temporal sharp activity. No recent bloods since December 2019; she is willing to attend for monitoring when feasible. 

Plan:  
- Continue Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily. No change given current stability.  
- Maintain ketogenic/modified regimen as above under external supervision; continue hydration and electrolyte support.  
- Arrange routine safety bloods via GP practice when service access allows: FBC, U&Es, LFTs, vitamin D, and Lamotrigine level (for baseline, not urgent).  
- Seizure diary to be continued; advise contacting our service if any breakthrough focal aware/impaired awareness events, new auras, or adverse medication effects occur.  
- Telephone follow-up in 3 months, sooner if problems arise. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 12-Apr-2020. The letter states the last seizure was on 19-Mar-2020 and she has been seizure-free since. The interval from 19-Mar to 12-Apr is about 24 days (~3.4 weeks). She is not seizure-free for ≥6 months, so we must calculate a frequency. Over approximately the last month, there has been 1 seizure, yielding an estimated current frequency of 1 per month.
seizure_frequency_number: Her last reported event was on 19-Mar and she has been seizure-free since.
